quote.msg:
純粹想入嚟表達
上年去完捷克都好鍾意呢個國家
加上好多本地人都好撐香港
布拉格個市長好似對疫情反應最快，又靚仔

replies.msg:
係呀 識嘅人都好關心同支持香港
所以雖然唔係大國 但覺得好幸福同感恩
布拉格市長真人更靚仔 非常貼地既一個人

去過Prague, Kutna Hora, Brno, CK, Karlovy Vary, Lipno, 同Hluboka Castle 等等地方(特別鍾意去啲好山旮旯嘅地方）

捷克我覺得係個一去便愛上嘅地方，幾年前去嘅時候都有衝動想移民過去，但見到幾年前嘅捷克相比其他歐洲地區嘅洗費相對地係低啲，但見到啲當地人都係好悠哉悠哉咁過生活，好羨慕。

除咗以上地方有無咩偏僻少人但風景靚嘅地方介紹？其實想問下多唔多香港人移民過去捷克？同埋嗰邊會唔會話會好歧視黃種人？你知㗎啦啲當地人一見到黃種人就覺得係大陸人。

Btw前幾年去見到好多韓國旅行團，嚇咗一跳

I have been to Czech last summer for exchange.
I am curious that many of them know Hong Kong and support us.
One time when I was eating in the restaurant, they said Hongkonger 'Gayau' in Cantonese, it's so warm
And it's only a small town in Czech.
Hope you like Hong kong too
will you leave hong kong if you were  a Hong konger.

Does Czech have any national value education in the cirriculum? If so, what is it like?

Do you learn to love the value of being a czech, trained to salute to the flag, etc?

I ask this because in Hong Kong schools are pushing forward a nationalistic education. I wonder what other countries do when it comes to national value education.

But, well, kids are learning to love a regime they dont actually belong to, a.k.a ,China.

quote.msg: Which city do you live?
in prague

I've been to Prague three times a beautiful city
Which city/town do you suggest us to visit other than Prague?

replies.msg: český krumlov is nice but too touristy. I would suggest Loket or Tábor it's small but still really nice especially in the historical centre

quote.msg:
I have been to Czech last summer for exchange.
I am curious that many of them know Hong Kong and support us.
One time when I was eating in the restaurant, they said Hongkonger 'Gayau' in Cantonese, it's so warm
And it's only a small town in Czech.
Hope you like Hong kong too

replies.msg: of course we do! we don't want hk to happen the same what happened to us in history when we were occupied by Russia. dark times

quote.msg: will you leave hong kong if you were  a Hong konger.

replies.msg:
that's really hard to answer. I would definitely consider it. situation in hk is really serious and its going to be even worse I guess..
I would at least try a new place and after while decided if I wanna stay there or go back.

quote.msg:
Does Czech have any national value education in the cirriculum? If so, what is it like?

Do you learn to love the value of being a czech, trained to salute to the flag, etc?

I ask this because in Hong Kong schools are pushing forward a nationalistic education. I wonder what other countries do when it comes to national value education.

But, well, kids are learning to love a regime they dont actually belong to, a.k.a ,China.

replies.msg: no we don't have anything like that. we don't salute to flag and it's really common to make a jokes from the politicians and even president. the one we have now is such a jerk. people here are open minded and they realise that government and politicians are there for people, paid by people not not vice versa
